title: Adoption Services - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > Adoption Services 54 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details A FAMILY FOR EVERY CHILD ADOPTION AGENCY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Adoption services for families adopting from foster care include training, home study certification, matching, placement, post-placement, finalization, post-finalization, support groups, and resource navigation 72C Centennial Loop Suite 100 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: People interested in adoption Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (541) 343-2856 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS AID FOSTER CARE AND ADOPTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Program Information Coordinator (PIC) provides information to families interested in foster care or adoption. Staff guides families through the certification and home study process for foster care and adoption programs. Foster parents receive ongoing training to maintain certification and support. 9320 SW Barbur Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: Foster parents must be age 21 or older and complete necessary training and application Hours: Family support: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Information sessions: -Infant adoption: 1st Tuesday of most months -Foster care adoption: 2nd Tuesday of the month Email (503) 222-9661 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHOICE ADOPTIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Non-denominational faith-based organization that provides information and referrals to those experiencing unplanned pregnancies. Assists with adoption process for those seeking to adopt and birth parents seeking child placement after delivery. Operates a 24/7 hotline to speak with advocates. Can provide birth parent with healthcare, educational support, and vocational resources. May also be able to assist with pregnancy related expenses such as housing, food, prenatal care, and maternity clothing. 12901 SE 97th Avenue Suite 150 Clackamas, OR 97015 Eligibility: Pregnant women considering adoption services Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm 24/7 Pregnancy Helpline Email (503) 232-1211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPEN ADOPTION & FAMILY SERVICES OPEN ADOPTION & FAMILY SERVICES EUGENE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports pregnant women and couples in making informed decisions about pregnancy options, including parenting, abortion, and adoption. Open adoptions offer ongoing visits and lifelong services. 1590 Willamette Street Suite 217 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Expectant parents Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm. On-call counselor available evenings and weekends for time-sensitive pregnancy options counseling and client work. Email (541) 343-4825 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPEN ADOPTION & FAMILY SERVICES OPEN ADOPTION & FAMILY SERVICES PORTLAND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports pregnant women and couples in making informed decisions about pregnancy options, including parenting, abortion, and adoption. Open adoptions offer ongoing visits and lifelong services. 9320 SW Barbur Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: Expectant parents Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm. On-call counselor available evenings and weekends for time-sensitive pregnancy options counseling and client work. The program also teaches people how to become resource parents. 5300 NE Elam Young Parkway Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, closed on major holidays (503) 693-4555 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON POST ADOPTION RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports Oregon adoptive families, state-assisted guardians, and adult adoptees with services like specialized consultations, supportive listening, resource referrals, and free training for families and the professionals who serve them. 6443 SW Beaverton Hillsdale Highway Suite 205 Portland, OR 97221 Eligibility: The organization provides full support to all adoptive and assisted guardianship families in Oregon, adult adoptees, and offers some services to resource families and the professionals who support them Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 241-0799 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES ODHS LOCAL OFFICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Locator tool to find out local information for ODHS offices. 500 Summer Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by County Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROAD TO HOPE MATERNITY HOUSING AND SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides stable, secure, and nurturing residential care in a home environment for pregnant women and women with children. Residents may stay at any point through their pregnancy and stay up to approximately one year after the birth of their baby. Residents have access to additional support with gaining OB/GYN care, high school/GED education completion, gaining employment, budgeting, individual counseling, advocacy and case management, developing parenting skills, and support working towards family reunification. Participation in faith-based worship service of choosing is required. Accepts mothers with one additional child, when capacity allows. Does not accept couples or individuals with pets of any kind. 2850 SW Cedar Hills Boulevard #55 Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Pregnant women age 18 or older who are in crisis (homeless, fleeing domestic violence, etc.). Women can be pregnant and have an additional child under the age of 3 depending on program capacity.
